title: agentx Tool SDK Schemas
description: Machine-validatable JSON Schema contracts for Volume 07 — Tool SDK. Covers Tool, ToolCategory, ToolCallContext, ToolResult, PermissionCheckRequest, PermissionCheckResult, and DestructiveClassification.

ToolCategory.description: Classification of tool by domain and risk level. Write/exec categories require approval.

Tool.description: A tool registered with the Tool SDK, available for agent invocation.

name.description: Canonical tool name, unique within the SDK registry.

isDestructive.description: Whether this tool can cause irreversible side effects. Destructive tools require operator approval.

parameters.description: JSON Schema describing the tool's input parameters.

version.description: Semantic version of the tool implementation.

ToolCallContext.description: Execution context passed to every tool invocation, enabling audit and access control.

agentRole.description: Role of the agent requesting the tool execution.

taskId.description: ID of the parent task that triggered this tool call.

workingDirectory.description: Absolute path of the working directory for the tool execution.

traceId.description: Distributed trace ID for observability correlation.

ToolResult.description: Standardized result returned by every tool execution.

success.description: Whether the tool execution completed without error.

output.description: Textual output or data produced by the tool.

error.description: Error message if the tool execution failed.

exitCode.description: Process exit code for shell-based tools.

PermissionCheckRequest.description: Request submitted to the permission guard before a tool is executed.

agentRole.description: Role of the agent requesting permission.

toolName.description: Name of the specific tool being invoked.

PermissionCheckResult.description: Result of a permission check — determines if the tool call proceeds or is blocked.

allowed.description: Whether the tool invocation is permitted.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation if the call was denied.

DestructiveClassification.description: Static classification of a tool's destructive potential, computed at registration time.

isDestructive.description: Whether this tool is classified as destructive.

rationale.description: Explanation for why the tool is or is not classified as destructive.
